steps.title: Create a one-year growth table
steps.content: The model starts at 15,000 people, so the initial value is $15000$. A $4%$ increase means each year multiplies the population by $1.04$.

steps.title: Read the model
steps.content: An exponential model has the form $p=a(b)^x$. Here $a=15000$ and $b=1.04$, so $$p=15000(1.04)^x.$$

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The model $p=15000(1.04)^x$ has the correct initial value, $15{,}000$, and growth factor, $1.04$. A factor of $0.96$ models a decrease, and using $15{,}000$ as the base instead of the coefficient changes the exponential model. D is correct.
